entities.name: AI Expert Elevation Principle
entities.context: Position that AI should move human experts higher in the problem stack rather than just automating low-level tasks

entities.name: Problem Stack
entities.context: Framework for understanding hierarchical levels of work from mechanical execution to strategic oversight

entities.name: Strategic Oversight
entities.context: Higher-order function including goal setting and objective alignment that becomes more valuable as foundational tasks are automated

entities.name: Mechanical Execution
entities.context: Lower-level tasks like editing that AI automation typically attacks first

entities.name: Professional Services Value Hierarchy
entities.context: Framework explaining that value is hierarchical and higher-order judgment increases in relative value as foundational costs approach zero

entities.name: Foundational Task Commoditization
entities.context: Pattern where the cost of executing foundational tasks approaches zero through automation

entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's company that must redesign workflows to focus on high-level strategy

entities.name: AI-Forward Service Providers
entities.context: Category of organizations that must intentionally redesign workflows or risk obsolescence

entities.name: Entry-Level Expertise Training Gap
entities.context: Challenge created when traditional training ground of low-level tasks is removed by automation

entities.name: Calculators
entities.context: Historical example of technology that moved mathematicians from arithmetic to theory

entities.name: Compilers
entities.context: Historical example of technology that moved programmers from machine code to system architecture

relationships.context: The principle frames human advancement in terms of moving higher in the problem stack
Strategic oversight is the target destination for elevated human experts
Mechanical execution is what should be automated rather than simply optimized
Value hierarchy reasoning supports why elevation is necessary
Commoditization creates obsolescence risk for providers who don't adapt
Careerspan must apply this principle to redesign workflows for high-level strategy
Historical precedent showing mathematicians moved from arithmetic to theory
Historical precedent showing programmers moved from machine code to architecture
Removing low-level tasks eliminates traditional training ground for new professionals
